session_summary: Marcus reframed the manuscript's credibility problem (a posture that presumes the reader's trust) and steered toward an IMRAD-with-explicit-Background structure, demanding concrete before/after examples before committing. He imposed a hard provenance rule (no weight-sourced citations; only manually-run Consensus searches feed the backlog), posed a conceptual challenge about why preregistration matters for a deductive result, and then offloaded Background drafting to the AI while he ran literature searches, repeatedly asking for prose to be printed/rendered for phone reading.

coded_turns.quote: The format still has a subtle posture of “I’m entitled to your trust because I have earned my membership to this community.” I’m thinking we need to rewrite in a IMRAD format
coded_turns.note: Diagnoses a credibility/posture problem in the manuscript and proposes IMRAD with an explicit trust-earning Background section.

coded_turns.quote: Give me examples of before and afters so this is concrete under your proposal. That way I can judge whether imrad is needed
coded_turns.note: Refuses to commit to IMRAD without concrete before/after examples; demands evidence before structural change.
coded_turns.artifact_hint: manuscript IMRAD decision gated on examples

coded_turns.quote: I agree with your analysis and let’s start with your suggestions. I’ll have a better idea if IMRAD is needed after
coded_turns.note: Approves the prose-fix + Background-carveout plan, defers the IMRAD-headings call until he can judge the draft.

coded_turns.quote: In both confortable with you using your weights to suggest citations.
coded_turns.note: Typo-laden assent (later reversed) tentatively allowing weight-sourced citation suggestions.

coded_turns.quote: In not confortable with you using your weights to suggest citations. Give me search queries I can manually run in consensus
coded_turns.note: Reverses prior turn; bans weight-sourced citations and requires manually-run Consensus searches instead.
coded_turns.artifact_hint: todo/003 rewritten as search-strategy entries (no named papers from weights)

coded_turns.quote: why preregistration matters when you have deductively proved something. It makes sense when induction is applied like in empirical studies.
coded_turns.note: Conceptual challenge: questions the rationale for preregistration on a deductively proved result vs inductive studies.
coded_turns.artifact_hint: verification-section justification (independence of inductive evidence vs theorem validity)

coded_turns.quote: While I work on getting consensus reports, I believe you can write the background?
coded_turns.note: Splits labor: Marcus runs Consensus searches while the AI drafts the Background section.

coded_turns.quote: You can do RAG using litrev_mcp.
coded_turns.note: Points the AI at litrev_mcp RAG so it can source the Rubin (1987) claim without opening the PDF.
coded_turns.artifact_hint: Rubin (1987) combining-rules citation via litrev RAG

coded_turns.quote: Shoe me the text here on the console
coded_turns.note: Asks the AI to print the drafted text to the console for review.

coded_turns.quote: I need you to print the intro and background here
coded_turns.note: Requests the intro and Background prose printed inline for direct reading.

coded_turns.quote: I’m reading in my phone. Please print the text in the intro and background tonight the consult so I can read it here in this conversation
coded_turns.note: Reiterates the inline-print request, citing phone reading as the constraint shaping the workflow.

coded_turns.quote: Can you render as a pdf so I can read here
coded_turns.note: Asks for a PDF render of the manuscript for phone reading.

ai_side.evidence: do you want full IMRAD headings, or the hybrid?
ai_side.note: Assistant put the IMRAD-vs-hybrid structural fork to Marcus rather than picking silently.

ai_side.evidence: the citation hook will block any that aren't backed in `literature/`. So if we go deep on Background, the acquisition of those references becomes a prerequisite
ai_side.note: Flagged that a richer Background forces reference acquisition as a hard prerequisite, not a parallel task.

ai_side.evidence: the `todo/003` block I added earlier names Nosek (2018) and Peng (2011) — those came from my weights, which is exactly what you're (rightly) ruling out
ai_side.note: Self-flagged its own weight-sourced citations as violating the rule Marcus had just set.

ai_side.evidence: will render as broken-citation markers** until the real references are acquired. That's the only thing standing between the current file and a clean render
ai_side.note: Flagged that the three @TODO placeholders will render as broken markers until Consensus picks land.

ai_side.evidence: Want me to render it to confirm nothing else broke (the three placeholders aside), or leave it until you've chosen the anchors?
ai_side.note: Offered to render or wait, leaving the call to Marcus.

ai_side.evidence: an LLM can fluently produce a wrong theorem *and* simulation code that appears to confirm it, because both came from the same possibly-wrong source — the "confirmation" is circular
ai_side.note: Conceded Marcus's deduction/induction distinction, recasting preregistration as guarding inductive-evidence independence, not theorem validity.
